stem: A food production study measures package weight for 15 samples to determine if the process variability is within ±5g of the nominal weight. The sample standard deviation is 4.37 grams (sample variance = 19.12). Estimate the true population standard deviation of package weight and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Sample SD = 4.37 grams. 95% CI for σ (chi-squared): [3.2, 6.9]. Point estimate: 4.37 grams.

common_failure_modes: Using z-based CI for the mean instead of χ²-based CI for the variance
Reporting the sample SD (4.37) as the population SD without uncertainty
Not recognizing that confidence intervals for variance are asymmetric
